vars.prSummary: Add email notification when order is completed. Registers a listener on the event bus and sends a confirmation email with order details to the customer.
vars.referenceCodeSuggestions:
[
  {
    "relevantFile": "src/services/notification.service.ts",
    "language": "typescript",
    "suggestionContent": "The event name 'order.completed' uses dot notation, but the emitter in order.events.ts uses colon notation ('order:completed'). The listener will never fire because the event names don't match. The codebase convention uses colons as separators.",
    "existingCode": "    this.eventBus.on('order.completed', async (payload) => {",
    "improvedCode": "    this.eventBus.on('order:completed', async (payload) => {",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Event listener uses wrong separator — 'order.completed' vs 'order:completed' — so the handler never fires",
    "relevantLinesStart": 28,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 28,
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "high"
  }
]

vars.prSummary: Add profile endpoint to return user email preferences. Adds GET /profile/:userId and GET /profile/:userId/avatar endpoints behind auth guard.
Implement session caching for faster auth. Creates sessions in Redis with TTL and provides invalidation methods for single and bulk session removal.
vars.referenceCodeSuggestions:
[
  {
    "relevantFile": "src/services/session.service.ts",
    "language": "typescript",
    "suggestionContent": "The session cache key uses colon separators (session:userId:token) but the auth middleware in auth.middleware.ts looks up sessions using underscore separators (session_userId_token). The middleware will never find sessions created by this service, causing all authenticated requests to fail with 401.",
    "existingCode": "    const cacheKey = `session:${userId}:${token}`;",
    "improvedCode": "    const cacheKey = `session_${userId}_${token}`;",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Cache key format mismatch — colons vs underscores — causes auth middleware to never find stored sessions",
    "relevantLinesStart": 38,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 38,
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "high"
  }
]

vars.prSummary: Add premium-only analytics dashboard access. Implements feature gating for the analytics dashboard — only Premium plan subscribers can provision and access custom dashboards.
vars.crossFileContextBlock:
### Codebase Context
The following snippets come from **other files in the repository** that interact with the code under review. Use them to detect cross-file issues (broken contracts, missing migrations, wrong assumptions).

### src/types/plan.type.ts (symbol: PlanType.PREMIUM)
**Rationale:** PlanType.PREMIUM was renamed to PlanType.PRO in a recent migration. PlanType.PREMIUM is undefined, so the comparison subscription.plan === PlanType.PREMIUM always fails, locking all premium/pro users out of the feature.
```
/**
 * Plan types for subscription tiers.
 *
 * MIGRATION NOTE (v3.1): Renamed PREMIUM -> PRO to align with
 * marketing rebrand. All database records have been migrated.
 * The PREMIUM value no longer exists in the enum.
 */
export enum PlanType {
  FREE = 'free',
  STARTER = 'starter',
  PRO = 'pro',           // formerly PREMIUM
  ENTERPRISE = 'enterprise',
}

export const PAID_PLANS: PlanType[] = [
  PlanType.STARTER,
  PlanType.PRO,
  PlanType.ENTERPRISE,
];
```
vars.referenceCodeSuggestions:
[
  {
    "relevantFile": "src/handlers/subscription.handler.ts",
    "language": "typescript",
    "suggestionContent": "PlanType.PREMIUM was renamed to PlanType.PRO in the v3.1 migration. PlanType.PREMIUM is now undefined, so the comparison `subscription.plan !== PlanType.PREMIUM` is always true (comparing against undefined), blocking all Pro-tier users from accessing analytics.",
    "existingCode": "    if (subscription.plan !== PlanType.PREMIUM) {\n      throw new ForbiddenException(\n        'Analytics dashboard is only available on the Premium plan. ' +\n        `Current plan: ${subscription.plan}`,\n      );\n    }",
    "improvedCode": "    if (subscription.plan !== PlanType.PRO) {\n      throw new ForbiddenException(\n        'Analytics dashboard is only available on the Pro plan. ' +\n        `Current plan: ${subscription.plan}`,\n      );\n    }",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "PlanType.PREMIUM no longer exists (renamed to PRO), so the guard always throws and blocks legitimate Pro users",
    "relevantLinesStart": 23,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 28,
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "high"
  }
]

vars.prSummary: Add report creation with timestamp tracking. Implements createReport, getReportsByDateRange, and updateReport methods with proper logging and ID generation.
vars.referenceCodeSuggestions:
[
  {
    "relevantFile": "src/services/report.service.ts",
    "language": "typescript",
    "suggestionContent": "createdAt and updatedAt are stored as ISO strings (via toISOString()), but the repository's findByDateRange uses MongoDB $gte/$lte operators comparing against Date objects. When createdAt is a string and the query parameter is a Date, MongoDB comparisons produce incorrect results — reports may be missing from or incorrectly included in date range queries.",
    "existingCode": "    const now = new Date().toISOString();

    const report: Report = {
      id: this.idGenerator.generate(),
      title: dto.title,
      organizationId: dto.organizationId,
      authorId: dto.authorId,
      content: dto.content,
      tags: dto.tags ?? [],
      createdAt: now,
      updatedAt: now,
    };",
    "improvedCode": "    const now = new Date();

    const report: Report = {
      id: this.idGenerator.generate(),
      title: dto.title,
      organizationId: dto.organizationId,
      authorId: dto.authorId,
      content: dto.content,
      tags: dto.tags ?? [],
      createdAt: now,
      updatedAt: now,
    };",
    "oneSentenceSummary": "Storing createdAt as ISO string breaks MongoDB date range queries which expect Date objects",
    "relevantLinesStart": 35,
    "relevantLinesEnd": 46,
    "label": "bug",
    "severity": "high"
  }
]
